title: Persea ovatiloba

intro: Persea ovatiloba, also known as the Korean perilla or Korean blue perilla, is a plant species belonging to the Lauraceae family, characterized by its ovate leaves and blue-purple flowers. The leaves of Persea ovatiloba have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to their potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Persea ovatiloba include rosmarinic acid, perillaldehyde, and perillene, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Persea ovatiloba include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions.
